_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize LOCAL batch: Hays County TX commissioner (walt-smith) + 4 San Marcos CISD trustees (halsey, shanks, cantu, cain). Cited, evidence-first; TX school-board races nonpartisan (party unknown). shanks LEFT office 2025 -> marked former.

profile.religion: Attends Austin Ridge Dripping Springs Church

walt-smith.notes_set: Republican Hays County Commissioner, Precinct 4 (Dripping Springs/Driftwood/Buda); first elected 2018, re-elected 2022. Announced Nov. 18, 2025 he will NOT seek re-election; term ends Dec. 31, 2026 (2026 Precinct 4 race open). Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17: affirmative pro-police advocacy (fought law-enforcement budget cuts; instituted sheriff school-outreach program). Deviations from rubric: amended-and-passed a 2025 LGBTQ Pride Month proclamation; sponsored/voted pro-immigrant due-process resolutions (humane treatment in custody; likened ICE arrests to Jan. 6) and helped 18 families gain citizenship; voted for property-tax-rate increases FY25 and FY26. No public positions found on life, family/schools, Christian liberty, election integrity, or 2A.

4.note: Did not oppose the June 10, 2025 LGBTQIA+ Pride Month proclamation; amended its language and let it pass -- contrary to opposing LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

0.note: Affirmative pro-law-enforcement advocacy: 'fought alongside our men and women of law enforcement and won in the face of massive proposed budget cuts'; instituted a Hays County Sheriff school-outreach program.

1.note: Championed and voted for a resolution affirming humane treatment/due process for immigrants in custody and stated he 'previously helped 18 families gain citizenship' -- contrary to supporting mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

2.note: Sponsored a June 24, 2025 resolution likening ICE arrests to Jan. 6 prosecutions and calling the enforcement 'unconscionable' -- actively resisted federal immigration enforcement, contrary to opposing sanctuary policy / supporting federal preemption.

anne-halsey.notes_set: San Marcos CISD At-Large trustee and Board President; serving since 2015 (TX school-board races are nonpartisan -- party unknown). Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17: documented positions lean against the rubric -- voted/acted to RETAIN 32 challenged library titles and defer to librarians (Dec. 2025); publicly aligned with LGBTQ+/'vulnerable student' inclusivity framing. Championed a 2024 Voter-Approved Tax Rate Election (tax-rate increase) while campaigning on property-tax cuts/debt paydown. No public positions found on life, marriage affirmation, Christian faith/prayer, election integrity, immigration, 2A, or affirmative police advocacy.

4.note: Publicly praised a colleague's commitment to an LGBTQ+-inclusive 'welcoming environment for everybody' and framed her board work around serving 'vulnerable' students -- contrary to opposing promotion of LGBTQ identity in schools.

2.note: At the Dec. 15, 2025 meeting the board approved 729 new library materials and RETAINED 32 challenged titles; Halsey deferred to librarians ('I don't think it's my job to read 729 books... trust... the librarians as the professionals') -- contrary to removing objectionable/SOGI library content.

brian-shanks.notes_set: FORMER San Marcos CISD District 4 trustee (served May 2022-May 2025). Did not refile in 2025; seat now held by John McGlothlin (filed unopposed, term ends 2028). Not a candidate in 2026. Marked former 2026-06-17. Only substantive citable record was fiscal: voted AGAINST placing the $166M May 2023 bond on the ballot (board passed 5-2), citing lack of long-term planning. No public positions found on social/moral rubric topics. (TX school-board races are nonpartisan.)

clementine-cantu.notes_set: San Marcos CISD District 5 trustee; long-tenured (first elected 2016), repeatedly unopposed; currently Board Vice President (John McGlothlin is now board president). TX school-board races are nonpartisan. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17: affirmatively backed school law enforcement (continued 5 SROs, 2022; supported armed school-marshal program, 2023). Deviation: as president, part of the 6-1 majority imposing a 2021 student/staff mask mandate defying the governor's order. No public positions found on life, marriage, family/curriculum, Christian liberty, election integrity, immigration, or 2A. (Note: the 'no books should be banned / TikTok' quote belongs to Samantha Cantu, a parent -- NOT this trustee.)

0.note: Affirmatively backed school law enforcement: supported continuing the district's 5 school resource officers (Aug. 2022) and supported the 6-1 approval of an armed school-marshal program (April 2023), suggesting 'retired law officers or retired military' serve as marshals.

2.note: As board president, part of the 6-1 majority that imposed a student/staff mask mandate (Aug. 12, 2021) defying Gov. Abbott's order banning such mandates -- imposed a mandate rather than protecting citizens from lockdown/mandate overreach.

profile.religion: Ordained ELCA Lutheran pastor -- pastor of Living Word Lutheran Church, Buda

jessica-cain.notes_set: San Marcos CISD District 1 trustee (first elected 2023; re-elected unopposed in 2026, term through 2029). TX school-board races are nonpartisan. Ordained ELCA Lutheran pastor (Living Word Lutheran, Buda); serves with Central Texas Interfaith and Texas Impact. Evidence-finalized 2026-06-17: credited for documented opposition to deficit spending (warned against a $2M-deficit compensation option, 2026). Deviations: campaign platform 'Anti book-banning'/'Pro public education'; voted to reject the SB763 school-chaplain program citing 'separation of church and state.' No public positions found on life, marriage affirmation, election integrity, police, immigration, or 2A.

2.note: Campaign platform explicitly lists 'Anti book-banning' alongside 'Pro public education' and 'Pro teachers' -- a documented stance against removing library/curricular content.

3.note: Voted with the board to reject the SB763 school-chaplain program, stating she was 'concerned about both qualifications and the separation of church and state' -- contrary to supporting prayer/Christian presence in public bodies.

2.note: Warned against a compensation option that 'were already putting ourselves at a $2 million deficit,' urging the board to 'be honest about the reality of our financial situation' -- documented opposition to deficit spending.
